routes.facadeSurface: No public `org.skia.paragraph` facade package exists in `:kanvas-skia`; only pure Kotlin paragraph owners exist today.
routes.targetContract: ParagraphInput, paragraph shaping requests, paragraph layout result, hit-testing, and selection dumps.
requiredEvidence: Public facade scope decision that names which paragraph-compatible entry points Kanvas exposes.
Checked-in paragraph dumps with hit-test and selection evidence.
Stable refusal diagnostics for unsupported shaping/fallback/color subcases.
routes.notes: Keep this route visible as missing facade work. Do not claim paragraph facade support from the pure Kotlin owner package alone.

routes.facadeSurface: Current path is a simple deterministic compatibility route through `SkFont.makeTextPath`; complex shaping remains explicit and separate.
routes.targetContract: Simple-text-only path building over pure Kotlin typeface/scaler facts with no implicit GSUB, bidi, paragraph, or fallback expansion.
requiredEvidence: Keep the `drawString` compatibility path explicitly simple and deterministic.
Separate any emoji/color/fallback promotion from complex shaping and GPU handoff tickets.
Retain stable refusal diagnostics when callers expect paragraph or explicit shaping behavior.
routes.notes: This route stays simple and deterministic by policy. It must not absorb explicit shaping, multi-font fallback splitting, or paragraph layout.

routes.facadeSurface: Metrics, glyph lookup, path/bounds, and positioning helpers already route through the attached `SkTypeface` hooks.
routes.targetContract: Typeface-backed metrics, glyph lookup, path/bounds, and positioning helpers over pure Kotlin scaler data.
requiredEvidence: Keep route-specific diagnostics stable when glyph outlines or metrics are unavailable.
Do not turn glyph query helpers into shaping or GPU route claims.
Document that any richer paragraph/shaping behavior is owned elsewhere.
routes.notes: This inventory row is about the bounded metrics/glyph-query route only; it is not a broad text-layout claim.

routes.facadeSurface: Abstract family/style discovery plus bundled OpenType managers; portable surface avoids platform font APIs in `:kanvas-skia/foundation`.
routes.targetContract: FontSourceID, TypefaceID, family/style catalog facts, source loading, and bounded fallback discovery.
requiredEvidence: Keep bundled/system scan behavior explicitly bounded and non-normative where host-dependent.
Do not imply platform fallback parity or native engine delegation.
Link catalog and source diagnostics to the pure Kotlin taxonomy before route promotion.
routes.notes: Current managers are reusable, but any support claim must stay bounded to the documented catalog/source route.

routes.facadeSurface: Explicit shaping boundary exists today, but it is still a compatibility layer and not the final pure Kotlin shaping engine.
routes.targetContract: ShapedGlyphRun, shaping-plan, fallback-run, and route-diagnostic dumps owned by the pure Kotlin shaping engine.
requiredEvidence: Advanced GSUB/GPOS, variation-adjustment, and required-script fixture families from M6.
Pure Kotlin fallback and cluster-safe shaping evidence attached to the explicit SkShaper route.
Stable shaping diagnostics that do not silently broaden `SkCanvas.drawString`.
routes.notes: Keep this route explicitly gated on shaping evidence. The current compatibility layer must not become a second shaping implementation.

routes.facadeSurface: Current explicit glyph-run rendering exists on CPU, but typed descriptor and no-`Sk*` handoff evidence is still missing from the facade route.
routes.targetContract: GPUGlyphRunDescriptor, GPUTextArtifactReference, and NormalizedDrawCommand.DrawTextRun with deterministic route diagnostics.
requiredEvidence: Typed glyph-run descriptor and artifact-reference dumps with no façade `Sk*` payload leakage.
Stable handoff diagnostics and stale/unregistered refusal evidence.
GPU evidence only when a GPU route is actually claimed.
routes.notes: Current CPU rendering is not enough to claim typed handoff readiness or `dftext` retirement.

routes.facadeSurface: Pure Kotlin OpenType-backed typefaces exist, but some high-level facts still expose `STUB.FONTATIONS` gaps and optional drift-only expectations.
routes.targetContract: TypefaceID plus SFNT table facts, variation facts, family-name iteration, and PostScript name access over pure Kotlin table data.
requiredEvidence: Route-specific parity dumps for PostScript name and localized family-name access.
Stable refusal or drift-only handling for any remaining external-engine comparison asks.
No hidden dependency on Fontations, FreeType, or platform name-table APIs.
routes.notes: Keep any Fontations/FreeType comparisons drift-only. The pure Kotlin route owns normative behavior.

legacyGateCoverage.rationale: Current color-path and bitmap-glyph compatibility behavior is still surfaced through `SkCanvas.drawString`, but blend/composite policy remains a tracked gate.
Typed glyph-run descriptors and artifact handoff are the M13 facade route that must stay visible before any `dftext` retirement claim.
Remaining `STUB.FONTATIONS` expectations live on the `SkTypeface` facts surface and must stay drift-only or expected-unsupported.
Any Fontations/FreeType comparison attached to `SkTypeface` remains optional drift evidence only.
The migration spec keeps PDF subset/embedding work adjacent to the runtime font facade. M13 inventory must keep the gate visible without pretending a facade route owns it.
Emoji sequence shaping and fallback affect explicit shaping first, with paragraph and compatibility routes remaining bounded and non-promoted.
Emoji rendering stays split across shaping, paragraph, and color/bitmap compatibility subroutes until dedicated evidence lands.

remainingGates: KFONT-M13-002 remains gated on route-specific `SkTypeface` parity evidence after this inventory.
KFONT-M13-003 remains gated on `KFONT-M6-010`; explicit `SkShaper` must not imply broader complex-shaping support.
KFONT-M13-004 remains gated on typed descriptor and GPU handoff readiness evidence after this inventory.
Paragraph-compatible facade scope is still missing from `:kanvas-skia`; no public `org.skia.paragraph` route exists yet.
`pdf_never_embed` remains adjacent/out-of-scope for the runtime facade and stays visible without a false owner-route claim.
